entries.definition:
dh–r–r small ants, specks, motes, powder; offspring; to sprinkle, to scatter, to cause to multiply, to spread creatures over the earth; to sprout. Of this root, three forms occur 38 times in the Qur’an: ذَرَّة dharratun six times; ذُرِّيَّة dhurriyyatun 28 times and ذُرِّيَّات dhurriyyāt four times.
ذَرَّة dharratun [n.] a mote, a speck, a very small ant (4:40) إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ God does not wrong [anyone] by [as much as] the weight of a speck of dust/an ant.

ذُرِّيَّة dhurriyyatun [n./coll. n.; pl. ذُرِّيَّات dhurriyyāt] offspring, descendants, progeny (7:172) وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِن بَنِي آدَمَ مِن ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ and remember when your Lord took from the children of Adam, from their loins, their seed.
